title: CY350 Lab 5 Website
text:
This website was created with Quarto for In-Class Lab 5. The goal of the lab is to build a simple website, render it locally, publish it online, and share the published URL with the instructor.






Note



This site is intentionally small: it demonstrates a home page, navigation, custom styling, and static HTML output that can be published with GitHub Pages or Netlify.

title: CY350 Lab 5 Website
text:
Network Concepts



Layer
Example
Why it matters




Application
HTTP, DNS
Defines the services users interact with


Transport
TCP, UDP
Moves application data between hosts


Network
IP, ICMP
Routes packets across networks


Link
Ethernet, Wi-Fi
Handles local network delivery

title: CY350 Lab 5 Website
text:
Lab Checklist

Created a Quarto website project.
Added custom content for CY350.
Rendered the site into static HTML.
Prepared the site for publishing.

title: CY350 Lab 5 Website
text:
Next Step
After publishing, submit the public website URL for credit.

title: About This Site
text: This Quarto website is a short demonstration for CY350 Computer Networks. It shows that the site can be written in .qmd files, rendered into HTML, and hosted as a static website.

title: About This Site
text: This Quarto website is a short demonstration for CY350 Computer Networks. It shows that the site can be written in .qmd files, rendered into HTML, and hosted as a static website.

title: About This Site
text:
Local Commands
From this lab folder:
quarto preview
quarto render
The rendered files are written to docs/, which is convenient for GitHub Pages if the repository is configured to publish from the main branch and /docs folder.

title: About This Site
text:
Publishing Options
The lab can be completed with either GitHub Pages or Netlify. GitHub Pages is a good fit for this folder because the rendered output is already in docs/. Netlify also works by publishing the rendered site directory.
